data.title: S&P 500 First-Quarter Profits Rise 28% Year Over Year, Analysts Flag Potential Volatility Risks
data.description:
S&P 500 profits rose 28% year over year in the first quarter, supporting a stronger outlook for stocks in the second half. According to NS3.AI, analysts said the earnings growth has improved expectations for the market’s performance later in the year.

Analysts also cautioned that several factors could increase volatility, including rising costs tied to artificial intelligence, inflation pressures, the possibility of additional Federal Reserve rate hikes, and the impact of new initial public offerings entering the market.

data.title: STOCKS | European Chip Stocks Fall 2%-4% in Early Trading
data.description: European chip stocks fell in early trading, with declines of 2% to 4% across several names. According to Jin10, ASML, ASM International, BE Semiconductor Industries (BESI), Infineon, and Aixtron were among the stocks posting losses in that range.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Optical Communications Shares Extend Declines, Cambridge Technology Down Over 23%
data.description:
Hong Kong-listed optical communications stocks continued to slide, with Cambridge Technology (06166.HK) falling more than 23%.

According to Jin10, Yangtze Optical Fibre and Cable (06869.HK) dropped more than 21%, while Hua Hong Hongli (01347.HK) and Lens Technology (06613.HK) also moved lower.

data.title: CPCA’s Cui Dongshu: Toyota Market Cap Fell 27% in June, Tesla Up 47% YoY
data.description:
Cui Dongshu, secretary-general of the China Passenger Car Association (CPCA), said in a July 1 post that global automakers’ market-cap moves in June were “brutal,” with overall valuations at the lowest level in 25 years. He said the market caps of international automakers such as Toyota and General Motors fell 7% year-on-year and 9% month-on-month, according to Jiemian News.

CPCA data covering more than 40 major automakers showed Toyota’s market cap fell 27% month-on-month in June and was down 12% year-on-year, hitting an 18-month low. Honda’s market cap fell 13% month-on-month and 22% year-on-year.

Toyota reported May 2026 global production of 857,765 vehicles, down 5.8% year-on-year, and global sales of 885,207, down 7.4%; its May sales in China were 102,300 vehicles, down 31.7%. Toyota has also said it will cut overseas output by about 100,000 vehicles by February 2027.

By contrast, Tesla’s market cap fell 4% month-on-month in June but rose 47% year-on-year, Cui said.

data.title: Nomura Asset Management Favors Taiwan and South Korean Stocks in Asia
data.description:
Nomura Asset Management said it is optimistic about Taiwan and South Korean equities among Asian markets.

According to Jin10, the firm stated that it favors Taiwan stocks and South Korean stocks within its Asia market view.

data.title: Binance to Distribute Micron (MU) Cash Dividend to MUB bStocks Holders and Suspend Conversions and Transfers
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the exchange will support the Micron (MU) cash dividend distribution via bStocks for users holding MUB. Binance said the net cash dividend amount will be reinvested, after applicable withholding taxes, fees, costs, and other deductions, into additional units or fractions of the same underlying security. Eligible users will receive dividends in the form of MUB bStocks, while on-chain holders of MUB will receive the bStock dividends through a multiplier adjustment. Binance added that general trading of MUB/USDT will not be affected. Dividends earned from assets held in other accounts will be credited to users’ Spot accounts, including holdings in accounts such as Savings and Margin. Binance said it will handle the technical requirements for users involved in the event.

Eligibility and Schedule

Binance said users with MUB holdings on the record date snapshot at 2026-07-06 00:00 (UTC) will be eligible for the dividend distribution. The exchange will suspend the Token Conversion service for 1:1 swaps between MU stock and MUB bStocks at 2026-07-02 23:45 (UTC). Binance will also suspend deposits and withdrawals of MUB tokens at 2026-07-05 23:45 (UTC), and Binance Convert will pause the conversion of MUB and all associated pairs at the same time. Binance said deposits, withdrawals, and Token Conversion service for MUB will resume when the distribution is completed, and no further announcement will be posted.

data.title: Metaplanet Adds 2,823 Bitcoin, Bringing Total Holdings to 43,000 BTC
data.description:
Metaplanet disclosed that it acquired an additional 2,823 Bitcoin, increasing its total holdings to 43,000 BTC.

According to NS3.AI, the company is described as a Japanese Bitcoin treasury company.

The disclosure indicates the purchase has already been executed as a treasury acquisition, rather than representing a planned allocation.

data.title: Energy Price Pullback Eases Switzerland’s Annual Inflation Rate to 0.5% in June
data.description:
Switzerland’s inflation rate edged down in June, marking its first decline since energy prices surged after the outbreak of the Iran war.

According to Jin10, the Swiss Federal Statistical Office said on Thursday that annual inflation was 0.5% in June, down from 0.6% in April and May. The previous two months had been the highest inflation readings since 2024.

Data showed that prices for petroleum products rose 15% year on year in June but eased from May. Air transport prices also fell, a category described as particularly sensitive to global energy prices. Meanwhile, hotel and car rental prices increased.

The modest pullback in inflation also aligned with comments by Swiss National Bank Chairman Schlegel. At last month’s monetary policy meeting, he said that while the Middle East conflict had triggered an energy price shock, medium-term inflation pressures remained broadly stable.

data.title: Quantum Systems Doubles Valuation to About $8 Billion After $1.2 Billion Funding Round
data.description:
Quantum Systems raised $1.2 billion in a funding round that more than doubled the German drone startup’s valuation to about $8 billion post-money, according to Bloomberg.

The deal is the latest example of investor enthusiasm for defense-related investments, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: India Food Regulator Issues Misbranding Notices to Energy-Drink Makers Including Red Bull Unit
data.description:
India’s food regulator issued notices to at least six energy-drink makers, including Red Bull GmbH’s local unit, over alleged misleading claims, according to Bloomberg.

The move tightens oversight of the rapidly expanding energy-drink category in the country.

data.title: Switzerland’s Inflation Slows for First Time in Eight Months
data.description: Swiss inflation slowed in June for the first time in eight months, suggesting lower oil costs are starting to feed through to the domestic economy, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: WTI Holds Near $65 as U.S.-Iran Talks Cool; Gold and Silver Volatility Rises Ahead of Jobs Data
data.description:
WTI crude stabilized around $65 after U.S.-Iran talks appeared to cool, while gold and silver saw amplified two-way swings ahead of the U.S. nonfarm payrolls release.

According to Jin10, the report said the easing momentum in U.S.-Iran negotiations coincided with WTI finding support below and rebounding toward $65.

It also said that on the eve of the nonfarm payrolls report, both gold and silver experienced larger-than-usual volatility in both directions.

data.title: AI | MGX Raises $49 Billion Fund for Artificial Intelligence Deals
data.description: MGX has raised $49 billion for one of the biggest-ever funds dedicated to artificial intelligence deals, propelling the two-year-old Abu Dhabi firm into the ranks of the most consequential investors in the sector globally, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: China’s Private Refiners Snap Up Middle East Oil as Prices Slide
data.description: China’s independent refiners are buying cheaper Middle Eastern crude after flows accelerated through the Strait of Hormuz, snapping up barrels from producers including Saudi Arabia and Iraq, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Hang Seng Indexes, Cyberport Launch Thematic Index Exploration Program
data.description: Hang Seng Indexes Company and Cyberport announced on June 30, 2026 the launch of the “Thematic Index Exploration Program” to work with innovation and technology companies to develop artificial intelligence solutions aimed at speeding up the design process for thematic indexes, according to Ming Pao.

data.title: Switzerland’s June CPI Monthly Rate Due for Release in 10 Minutes
data.description:
Switzerland’s June consumer price index (CPI) monthly rate was due to be released in 10 minutes.

According to Jin10, the data release was expected shortly, but no figures were provided in the report.

data.title: STOCKS | Australia’s S&P/ASX 200 Ends Slightly Lower at 8,717.40
data.description:
Australia’s S&P/ASX 200 index closed down 5.50 points, or 0.06%, at 8,717.40 on July 2 (Thursday).

According to Jin10, the benchmark finished the session slightly lower.

data.title: STOCKS | PwC Sees Hong Kong IPO Fundraising at HK$380 Billion in 2026, Could Top HK$400 Billion
data.description:
PwC expects Hong Kong’s IPO market to raise about HK$380 billion in 2026 and said fundraising could exceed HK$400 billion depending on market conditions, according to Ming Pao.

Wong Kam-chin, PwC’s capital markets services lead partner, said at a “2026 Hong Kong IPO Market Mid-Year Review and Outlook” press conference that he remains confident about growth despite short-term challenges including geopolitical tensions, inflation, and interest-rate volatility. PwC also raised its full-year forecast for the number of new listings to 150.

data.title: Solana Rises 15.8% Weekly After World Prediction Market Launches in Phantom
data.description:
Solana rose 15.8% over the past week after a Solana-based prediction market called World launched inside Phantom. According to NS3.AI, CoinGecko data also shows SOL gained nearly 5% in the last 24 hours.

The update links SOL’s recent price strength to the launch of World within the Phantom wallet ecosystem.

data.title: Hong Kong SFC Reports 20% Rise in AUMto HK$42.2 Trillion in 2025
data.description: Hong Kong’s Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) released its 2025 Asset and Wealth Management Activities Survey, reporting that total assets under management rose 20% year on year to a record HK$42.2 trillion.According to Odaily, the SFC said the latest figure surpassed the previous high of HK$35.5 trillion recorded in 2021.The survey attributed part of the increase to net fund inflows, which rose 193% from a year earlier to HK$2.1 trillion, marking the third consecutive year of growth.By segment, assets under management in asset management and fund advisory businesses increased 19% year on year to HK$31 trillion. Assets under management in private banking and private wealth management rose 24% to HK$12.9 trillion.
